target.name: Palantir
target.founder: Peter Thiel, Alex Karp, Stephen Cohen, Joe Lonsdale, Nathan Gettings

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $126.0B - $300.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Palantir is a public-stage AI & Software company with a current valuation exceeding $400 billion. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of nine comparable companies: one public entity (Palantir itself) and eight private frontier-tech firms including OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, Databricks, and Waymo.

The peer-median approach anchors the analysis. Five same-sector peers with similar valuation tiers—OpenAI ($300.0B), xAI ($250.0B), Anthropic ($183.0B), Databricks ($134.0B), and Waymo ($126.0B)—yield a median valuation of $183.0B, with a range spanning $126.0B to $300.0B. This method is applicable given the homogeneity of the comparison set across sector and scale.

The triangulated analysis, constrained by the single applicable methodology, supports a valuation range of $155.6B to $210.4B, with a midpoint of $183.0B. The current $400B+ valuation materially exceeds this derived range. Key uncertainties include the limited methodological breadth—only peer-median triangulation is applicable—and reliance on private valuations for the comp set, which may not reflect realized exit prices or public market discipline. The substantial gap between implied and current valuation warrants inquiry into recent operating performance, revenue growth rates, or market-specific risk premiums not captured in the peer set.
